title: Sarga 102
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Having heard that, Rama, along with his brothers, felt great joy and then spoke amazing words to Lakshmana.” and develops toward “Lakshmana, the son of Sumitra, followed Angada, and Bharata became Chandraketu's close supporter.”

This passage begins with “Lakshmana, having resided for a year in Angada's territory until his unconquerable son was established, then returned to Ayodhya.” and develops toward “Having spent their time with fulfilled desires, endowed with prosperity, and well-established in the city of dharma, the three brothers shone like…”

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा हर्षमापेदे राघवो भ्रातृभिः सह । वाक्यं चाद्भुतसङ्काशं तदा प्रोवाच लक्ष्मणम् ।। 7.102.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that, Rama, along with his brothers, felt great joy and then spoke amazing words to Lakshmana.

verses.sanskrit: इमौ कुमारौ सौमित्रे तव धर्मविशारदौ । अङ्गदश्चन्द्रकेतुश्च राज्यार्थे दृढविक्रमौ ।। 7.102.2 ।।
verses.translation: O Lakshmana, these two sons of yours, Angada and Chandraketu, are skilled in righteousness and possess firm valor suitable for kingship.

verses.sanskrit: इमौ राज्ये ऽभिषेक्ष्यामि देशः साधु विधीयताम् । रमणीयो ह्यसम्बाधो रमेतां यत्र धन्विनौ ।। 7.102.3 ।।
verses.translation: I shall enthrone these two in a kingdom, so let a suitable, charming, and unobstructed region be prepared where these two archers can happily reside.

verses.sanskrit: न राज्ञो यत्र पीडा स्यान्नाश्रमाणां विनाशनम् । स देशो दृश्यतां सौम्य नापराध्यामहे यथा ।। 7.102.4 ।।
verses.translation: O gentle one, let a region be found where there is no oppression of kings or destruction of hermitages, so that we may not commit any fault.

verses.sanskrit: तथोक्तवति रामे तु भरतः प्रत्युवाच ह । अयं कारुपथो देशो रमणीयो निरामयः ।। 7.102.5 ।।
verses.translation: When Rama had thus spoken, Bharata replied, "This region called Karupatha is charming and peaceful."

verses.sanskrit: निवेश्यतां तत्र पुरमङ्गदस्य महात्मनः । चन्द्रकेतोः सुरुचिरं चन्द्रकान्तं निरामयम् ।। 7.102.6 ।।
verses.translation: Let a city be established there for the great-souled Angada and another, very beautiful, moon-charming, and healthy city for Chandraketu.

verses.sanskrit: तद्वाक्यं भरतेनोक्तं प्रतिजग्राह राघवः । तं च कृत्वा वशे देशमङ्गदस्य न्यवेशयत् ।। 7.102.7 ।।
verses.translation: Rama accepted that statement spoken by Bharata, and having brought that region under control, he established a city for Angada.

verses.sanskrit: अङ्गदीया पुरी रम्याप्यङ्गदस्य निवेशिता । रमणीया सुगुप्ता च रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ।। 7.102.8 ।।
verses.translation: The beautiful city named Angadiya was established for Angada by Rama, whose deeds are effortless, and it was delightful and well-protected.

verses.sanskrit: चन्द्रकेतोश्च मल्लस्य मल्लभूम्यां निवेशिता । चन्द्रकान्तेति विख्याता दिव्या स्वर्गपुरी यथा ।। 7.102.9 ।।
verses.translation: And for Chandraketu, a city named Chandrakanta was established in the land of Malla, renowned as divine, just like a city of heaven.

verses.sanskrit: ततो रामः परां प्रीतिं लक्ष्मणो भरतस्तथा । ययुर्युद्धे दुराधर्षा अभिषेकं च चक्रिरे ।। 7.102.10 ।।
verses.translation: Then Rama, Lakshmana, and Bharata, who were unconquerable in battle, attained great joy and performed the anointment.

verses.sanskrit: अभिषिच्य कुमारौ स प्रस्थापयति राघवः । अङ्गदं पश्चिमां भूमिं चन्द्रकेतुमुदङ्मुखम् ।। 7.102.11 ।।
verses.translation: Having consecrated the two princes, Rama sent Angada to the western region and Chandraketu to the northern direction.

verses.sanskrit: अङ्गदं चापि सौमित्रिर्लक्ष्मणो ऽनुजगाम ह । चन्द्रकेतोस्तु भरतः पार्ष्णिग्राहो बभूव ह ।। 7.102.12 ।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana, the son of Sumitra, followed Angada, and Bharata became Chandraketu's close supporter.

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्मणस्त्वङ्गदीयायां संवत्सरमथोषितः । पुत्रे स्थिते दुराधर्षे अयोध्यां पुनरागमत् ।। 7.102.13 ।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana, having resided for a year in Angada's territory until his unconquerable son was established, then returned to Ayodhya.

verses.sanskrit: भरतो ऽपि तथैवोष्य संवत्सरमतो ऽधिकम् । अयोध्यां पुनरागम्य रामपादावुपास्त सः ।। 7.102.14 ।।
verses.translation: Bharata also, having similarly resided for more than a year, returned to Ayodhya and worshipped Rama's feet.

verses.sanskrit: उभौ सौमित्रिभरतौ रामपादावनुव्रतौ । कालं गतमपि स्नेहान्न जज्ञाते ऽतिधार्मिकौ ।। 7.102.15 ।।
verses.translation: Both Lakshmana and Bharata, being extremely righteous and devoted to Rama's feet, did not even perceive the passage of time due to their profound affection.

verses.sanskrit: एवं वर्षसहस्राणि दश तेषां ययुस्तदा । धर्मे प्रयतमानानां पौरकार्येषु नित्यदा ।। 7.102.16 ।।
verses.translation: Thus, ten thousand years passed for them, who were constantly striving in righteousness and civic duties.

verses.sanskrit: विहृत्य कालं परिपूर्णमानसाः श्रिया वृता धर्मपुरे सुसंस्थिताः । त्रयः समिद्धा इव दीप्ततेजसो महाध्वरे साधु हुतास्त्रयो ऽग्नयः ।। 7.102.17 ।।
verses.translation: Having spent their time with fulfilled desires, endowed with prosperity, and well-established in the city of dharma, the three brothers shone like three well-kindled fires of blazing splendor, properly offered in a great sacrifice. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे व्द्युत्तरशततमः सर्गः ।। 102 ।। Thus ends the hundred and second sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
